summary: Completed comprehensive testing of AWS Macie PII Detection feature. All 25 backend API tests pass (100%). Frontend dashboard renders correctly with all 5 tabs (Overview, Findings, Scan Jobs, Custom Identifiers, S3 Buckets) working. CRUD operations for jobs and custom identifiers verified. Sample data auto-created on service initialization.
critical_code_review_comments: All Macie endpoints properly return data with correct status codes
Sample data includes 6 findings, 4 jobs, 4 custom identifiers, and 6 S3 buckets
Findings support severity filtering (High/Medium/Low) and acknowledgement status filtering
Custom identifiers support full CRUD operations with regex patterns and keywords
Dashboard stats aggregate all metrics correctly
All interactive elements have proper data-testid attributes for testing
AWS Macie API calls are MOCKED/SIMULATED - no real AWS connection
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_macie.py - Created comprehensive test suite with 25 tests
seed_data_creation: Sample data auto-created on service initialization: 6 findings (4 High, 1 Medium, 1 Low severity), 4 classification jobs (1 RUNNING, 3 COMPLETE), 4 custom identifiers (Employee ID, Artist Contract Number, Royalty Account ID, Internal Project Code), 6 S3 buckets (bme-artist-contracts, bme-royalty-data, bme-hr-records, bme-marketing, bme-dev-backups, bme-public-assets). UI tests created additional TEST_ prefixed jobs and identifiers.
context_for_next_testing_agent: AWS Macie PII Detection is fully functional. Access via /macie route after login. Features tested: health check, dashboard stats, finding statistics, findings (list, filter by severity/acknowledged, acknowledge), jobs (list, filter by status, create), custom identifiers (CRUD), buckets (list), PII types reference, severity levels reference. Frontend tabs all working: Overview (alert banner, stats cards, PII types detected, recent scan activity, high severity findings), Findings (severity/status filters, acknowledge button), Scan Jobs (status filters, job cards), Custom Identifiers (identifier cards with regex), S3 Buckets (bucket cards with security info). Create Job and Create Identifier modals both functional.

mocked_apis_list: AWS Macie enable_macie - Not connected to real AWS
AWS Macie create_classification_job - Creates local job record in MongoDB
AWS Macie create_custom_data_identifier - Creates local identifier record in MongoDB
AWS Macie get_findings - Returns sample findings from MongoDB
mocked_apis.note: AWS Macie API calls are MOCKED/SIMULATED. No real AWS Macie connection - sample findings and jobs are generated locally and stored in MongoDB.

data_verified.findings: 6 sample findings (4 High: SSN/Financial/Credentials/Medical, 1 Medium: Email/Phone/Name, 1 Low: Email/Phone)
data_verified.jobs: 4 sample jobs (Weekly Full Scan, Marketing Data Audit, Dev Environment Check, Continuous Monitoring)
data_verified.custom_identifiers: 4 sample identifiers (Employee ID, Artist Contract Number, Royalty Account ID, Internal Project Code)
data_verified.buckets: 6 S3 buckets (bme-artist-contracts, bme-royalty-data, bme-hr-records, bme-marketing, bme-dev-backups, bme-public-assets)
data_verified.pii_types: 14 PII types (SSN, Credit Card, Bank Account, Email, Phone, Passport, Driver's License, DOB, Medical Record, IP Address, Name, Address, AWS Secret Key, AWS Access Key ID)
